primary.title: Primary Sit — Core Converge
primary.reasoning: Win strength: Narrow. Breaks if: movement loads harder into the backup lane than this shelf. State Game Land 204 stays legal here; 695 m on a upper third bench. Primary read: Bench Bedding Edge. Support: terrain continuity. This sit is level with the surrounding pull with a flatter shelf on the side-hill, which creates a more believable hunting setup than a flat random point. It also holds better interior security than a boundary-hugging sit. Species tuning: Whitetail Deer modifier +18.0. Feature-aware signals: bench, cover (+9.0). Whitetail will favor low-pressure edges, bedding cover, mast or crop food, wind-safe access, and terrain funnels that let deer move without exposing themselves. Vegetation read: shrub cover adds workable concealment and modestly strengthens travel and bedding confidence. This read came from terrain fallback rather than direct land-cover data, so confidence stays slightly tempered. Cover proxy: thicker-feeling travel structure adds security to movement here. Feeding proxy: bench-edge and lower-shelf terrain adds a mild destination pull. Competitive read: this is a narrow win over the nearest backup because it carries slightly cleaner total terrain fit. Conditional read: this stays viable unless movement loads harder into the backup lane than this shelf.

notes: Selected bbox is enforced as a hard analysis fence before ranking and viewer payload generation.
Only legal areas can surface primary or alternate sits when verified legal candidates exist.
Restricted and unknown areas remain visible as suppressed near-misses.
Ranking now adds relative terrain trust logic so convergence, shoulder, funnel, bench, and edge reads come from the DEM instead of presentation-only phrasing.
Ranking blends terrain structure, legal gating, timing fit, and wind guard logic.
When no verified legal candidates exist inside the selected bbox, the system falls back to terrain-only review mode instead of crashing.
Provider health now reduces confidence and score when terrain or legal data is degraded.
Vegetation classification now meaningfully changes concealment, bedding confidence, and weak-cover penalties inside sit scoring.

species_profile.label: Whitetail Deer
species_profile.positive_hook: Whitetail will favor low-pressure edges, bedding cover, mast or crop food, wind-safe access, and terrain funnels that let deer move without exposing themselves.
species_profile.caution_hook: For whitetail, downgrade easy-looking spots when wind, human pressure, access exposure, or legal confidence is weak.

invalidators: Wind drifts into the preferred approach lane.
Access pressure rises on the legal edge.
The route forces you through the hot corridor instead of offsetting from it.
Terrain quality or legal verification is too weak to trust the sit call.

notes: Terrain layer fetched from the preferred real provider path.

Wildlife atmosphere layer generated BBOX-driven species and method context without touching the 3D terrain core.
Decision layer consumed DEM-derived terrain structure and legal gating.
Command surface rendered a terrain-first operator view with intelligence controls.
Heuristic fallback: mid-scale terrain window biased toward shrub or mixed cover.
Legal land output is an aid, not final authority. Always verify land access, ownership, boundaries, and hunting legality in the real world before hunting.
